meta.source: vignanam.org — IAST transliteration, corrected against Chris Hughes lineage
meta.notes: v1.1: Parameshvara-Parameshvari combined; Uddiishamayi added; Kaalataapaana corrected; Garima Siddhe removed; translation and note fields added.

scripts.iast: ōṃ aiṃ hrīṃ śrīṃ aiṃ klīṃ sauḥ ōṃ namastripurasundarī
scripts.devanagari: ओं ऐं ह्रीं श्रीं ऐं क्लीं सौः ओं नमस्त्रिपुरसुन्दरी
scripts.english: Om Aim Hrim Shrim Aim Klim Sauh Om Namas Tripura Sundari
scripts.marathi: ओं ऐं ह्रीं श्रीं ऐं क्लीं सौः ओं नमस्त्रिपुरसुन्दरी
scripts.hindi: ओं ऐं ह्रीं श्रीं ऐं क्लीं सौः ओं नमस्त्रिपुरसुन्दरी
scripts.telugu: ఓం ఐం హ్రీం శ్రీం ఐం క్లీం సౌః ఓం నమస్త్రిపురసున్దరీ
scripts.tamil: ஓம் ஐம் ஹ்ரீம் ஶ்ரீம் ஐம் க்லீம் ஸௌஃ ஓம் நமஸ்த்ரிபுரஸுந்தரீ
scripts.kannada: ಓಂ ಐಂ ಹ್ರೀಂ ಶ್ರೀಂ ಐಂ ಕ್ಲೀಂ ಸೌಃ ಓಂ ನಮಸ್ತ್ರಿಪುರಸುನ್ದರೀ
scripts.malayalam: ഓം ഐം ഹ്രീം ശ്രീം ഐം ക്ലീം സൌഃ ഓം നമസ്ത്രിപുരസുംദരീ

scripts.translation: The Divine God and Divine Goddess

deities.note: Chanted as a pair — the primordial divine couple
Mitrēśa ('lord of friends') is a form of Śiva; second in the divine lineage
Ṣaṣṭhīśa is a form of Śiva; third in the divine lineage
Caryānātha is a master revered in both Śaiva and Nātha traditions; fifth in the divine lineage
Wife of Agastya and a great adept in her own right; co-revealer of Śrī Vidyā and author of hymns in the Ṛgveda
Vedic sage and one of the Saptaṛṣis; widely credited with transmitting Śrī Vidyā to South India
Kālatāpana means 'one who burns away time'; first of the perfected (siddha) lineage
Dharmācārya means 'teacher of dharma'; second of the perfected lineage
Muktakēśīśvara means 'lord of unbound hair'; third of the perfected lineage
Dīpakalānātha means 'lord of the art of light'; fourth of the perfected lineage
First of the human lineage masters
Prabhākara means 'he who makes radiant'; second of the human lineage
Tējas means 'radiance' or 'splendour'; third of the human lineage
Manōja means 'born of the mind'; fourth of the human lineage
Kalyāṇa means 'auspicious' or 'well-being'; fifth of the human lineage
Vāsudēva is a name of Viṣṇu-Kṛṣṇa meaning 'son of Vasudeva'; seventh of the human lineage
Ratna means 'jewel' or 'gem'; sixth of the human lineage
The final human teacher in the lineage, through whom the living tradition reaches the practitioner

scripts.translation: Enchantress of the Three Worlds — Presiding Deity
Fulfiller of All Hopes — Presiding Deity
Bestower of All Good Fortune — Presiding Deity
Maker of All that is Beloved
Accomplisher of All Goals — Presiding Deity
Remover of All Disease — Presiding Deity
Bestower of All Perfections — Presiding Deity
The Great Empress — Lalita Mahatripura Sundari
Beautiful Goddess of the Three Worlds
Garlanded Goddess of the Three Worlds
Perfected Goddess of the Three Worlds
The Great Beautiful Goddess of the Three Worlds

scripts.english: Maha Maha Shri Chakra Nagara Samrajni
scripts.translation: Great Great Empress of the Shri Chakra City

lines.label: Horizontal mid-line (left to right)
lines.note: Deities aligned horizontally across circuits 4–7. Positions TBD — confirm against SVG geometry.

lines.label: Vertical mid-line (bottom to top)
lines.note: Deities aligned vertically across circuits 4–7. Positions TBD — confirm against SVG geometry.
